decision: Expand active Greek Daniel from string-only NRSV rows to explicit translation objects, then populate KJV and NABRE only for mapped addition rows where source witnesses are bound.
reason: KJV and NABRE source witnesses are bound and address-mapped for Prayer/Song, Susanna, and Bel and the Dragon.
Active danielGK currently stores string text, which represents NRSV but is not an explicit NRSV lane.

The Bible browser treats string verse.text as available text for any requested translation, so adding KJV/NABRE metadata without object conversion would risk displaying NRSV as KJV or NABRE for rows where those lanes are absent.
Converting existing strings to text.NRSV preserves the active NRSV text while making lane absence explicit.

repairScope.convertAllStringRows: Every existing string verse.text in danielGK becomes { NRSV: existingString }.
repairScope.populateKJV: Populate KJV only for active addition rows mapped from bound KJV source files: Daniel 3:24-90, Daniel 13:1-64, Daniel 14:1-42.
repairScope.populateNABRE: Populate NABRE only for active addition rows mapped from bound historical NABRE Daniel source: Daniel 3:24-90, Daniel 13:1-64, Daniel 14:1-42.
doNotPopulate: KJV ordinary Daniel rows in danielGK during this repair
NABRE ordinary Daniel rows in danielGK during this repair
NRSV additions from Oremus or BibleGateway candidates

metadataRule: Add meta.translations entries only for lanes actually represented after repair: NRSV, KJV, NABRE. Do not add DRB or Rotherham here.
nextRequiredWork: Run bounded lane-expansion repair script locally.
Run collation-status audit again; KJV/NABRE addition lanes should be present.
Run exact KJV/NABRE addition collation audit after lane expansion.
